title: Chicken Karaage - Double-Fry Method
description: The secret is cornstarch in the marinade and a double-fry for that satisfying crunch. Serve with Kewpie mayo.
alt_text: Crispy chicken karaage on a plate with Kewpie mayo

title: Japanese Curry from Scratch - S&B vs Homemade Roux
description: S&B medium is the reliable pick for weeknight cooking. Potato, carrot, onion, chicken thigh. The secret: grated apple and dark chocolate finish.
alt_text: Bowl of Japanese curry rice with sliced chicken

title: Bibimbap - Stone Bowl vs Regular Bowl
description: Dolsot (stone bowl) bibimbap gives you the crispy rice bottom. Worth the investment if you make it more than twice a month.
alt_text: Colorful bibimbap in a stone bowl with gochujang

title: Haemul Pajeon - Korean Seameal Scallion Pancakes
description: Crispy edges, chewy center, loaded with shrimp and scallion. Dipping sauce: soy + rice vinegar + sesame oil + gochugaru.
alt_text: Korean seameal pancake with dipping sauce

title: Kootenai Canyon Climbing Area - Bitterroot Valley MT
description: Sport and trad routes on limestone. 35 min from Missoula. Go early on hot days - south-facing but shaded until 10am. Watch for rattlesnakes May-October.
alt_text: Limestone cliffs in Kootenai Canyon with a climber

title: Evolv Shaman Review - Best Aggressive Shoe for Steep Bouldering?
description: Stiff, asymmetric, downturned. Brutal on stand-up V4 slabs but outstanding on overhung compression problems. Size down 1.5.
alt_text: Evolv Shaman climbing shoe pair

title: Hangboard Training Plan for Intermediate Climbers
description: Max hangs vs. repeaters vs. density. If you're projecting V4-V6 this periodization template from Lattice Training is solid.
alt_text: Hangboard fingerboard training guide infographic

title: Glacier NP in December - What to Expect
description: Going-to-the-Sun Road closes to Avalanche Creek in winter. Lake McDonald area is accessible. Cell service near zero. Offline maps essential (Gaia GPS).
alt_text: Snow-covered mountains reflected in Lake McDonald, Glacier NP

title: Blue Mountain Trail - Missoula MT
description: Northridge trail: 8 miles RT, 1,200ft gain. Best in October for fall color. Moderate difficulty. Great views of Missoula valley from the summit.
alt_text: Blue Mountain trail above Missoula in fall foliage

title: 3-Day Backcountry Meal Plan - High Calorie, Low Weight
description: Japanese curry with instant rice, soba + miso, Clif bahrs, trail mix, and freeze-dried backup. 2,600 kcal/day at ~1.8 lb/day.
alt_text: Backcountry meal kit laid out on tent ground cloth

title: Pain Neuroscience Education - Explain Pain Book Summary
description: Pain is an output of the brain, not a readout of tissue damage. Central sensitization, fear-avoidance, and graded exposure are the pillars. Moseley & Butler 2013.
alt_text: Infographic summarizing pain neuroscience education concepts

title: ACT Hexaflex for chronic pain group - Professional Application
description: Acceptance + defusion + present moment + self-as-context + values + committed action. For pacing: 'your capacity varies and that's okay' is a values-based flexibility intervention.
alt_text: ACT hexaflex diagram adapted for chronic pain group therapy

title: goal measure - Canadian Occupational Performance Measure
description: Gold-standard client-centered outcome measure. Semi-structured interview: identify occupational performance issues, rate importance, self-rate performance and satisfaction. Used in HTCC prep.
alt_text: goal measure assessment form and scoring guide for Occupational Therapy

title: Homemade Miso Fermentation Guide - Shiro vs. Mugi
description: Shiro (white) miso: sweeter, shorter ferment (3-6 weeks). Mugi (farro) miso: deeper umami, 6+ months. The koji ratio determines everything.
alt_text: Ceramic crock with homemade miso at different fermentation stages

title: Open-Hand vs. Crimp Grip - When to Use Each
description: Open-hand is safer for finger health and develops better tendon strength long-term. Full crimp for performance on small holds only. Mix 80/20.
alt_text: Diagram showing open-hand vs. crimp grip positions on rock holds

title: Lake McDonald in Winter - Driving Guide
description: US-2 stays open to Apgar/Lake McDonald Lodge area. GTSR closed past Avalanche Creek. No gas in West Glacier in winter - fill in Whitefish.
alt_text: Winter road approaching Lake McDonald Lodge with snow on trees

title: Tonkotsu Ramen from Scratch - 12-Hour Broth
description: Blanch then roast bones. 12hr simmer at a roll. The milky-white emulsification is the goal. Tare options: shio, shoyu, or miso.
alt_text: Bowl of tonkotsu ramen with chashu pork and soft-boiled egg

title: Farrar et al. 2001 - NRS Minimal professionally Important Difference
description: 2-point change on 0-10 NRS = MCID for chronic pain group. N=2,724 across 10 RCTs. The gold standard for interpreting pain score changes in professional trials and app outcomes.
alt_text: Summary card for Farrar 2001 NRS MCID professional significance threshold

title: Rattlesnake Wilderness Bouldering - Hidden Gems
description: Granite boulders scattered throughout the lower Rattlesnake. Best in spring and fall. Good V2-V4 range. Bring tick marks and a crash pad.
alt_text: Granite boulder problem in Rattlesnake Wilderness with fall colors

title: Bob Marshall Wilderness Complex - Planning Guide
description: The Bob: 1.5 million acres. Entry via Seeley Lake, Lincoln, or Spotted Bear. Permit-free but bear canisters required June-Nov. Snow possible any month.
alt_text: Map overview of the Bob Marshall Wilderness with major trails marked
